notes:
Worked like a charm after some tweaks.

To be able to run the game in full screen, I had to configure it using Settings.exe in the installation folder. This doesn't run without further tweaking. So I did the following:
0.) I started the game once to get basic configuration folders created.  
1.) I created another wineprefix and used the following winetricks (using wine 4.5 staging): dotnet472, vcrun2015, allfonts  
2.) I started the Settings.exe in that wineprefix using wine 4.5 (haven't tried doing so using proton, might also work). Then I was able to set up the configuration.  
3.) I copied the created settings.dat to proton's wineprefix ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/592580/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/KONAMI/PRO EVOLUTION SOCCER 2018  
4.) I started the game by steam launcher using Proton 4.2.2. It ran perfectly at around 120 fps.
title: PRO EVOLUTION SOCCER 2018

notes: Out of the box, the game runs in window mode. Fixing this requires either copying settings.dat from a Win installation. Or generating it using Settings.exe under Proton/Wine which requires several winetricks to make it run: dotnet45, allfonts. Works like a charm afterwards, including controller (XBox One via Bluetooth) and team modifications (PESGalaxy, installers working out of the box under Proton/Wine).
title: PRO EVOLUTION SOCCER 2018

notes: Runs out of box but for change settings need some additional work. For change settings like full screen mode I recommend create new wine 64bit prefix then install dotnet472 (Skip all errors about 32 bit prefix and every else) after this run settings.exe inside pes directory. When we change settings by clicking OK. The program will create settings.dat under KONAMI directory in our linux Document folder all what we need to do is copy this file and past it to Document's inside PES prefix created by steam.
title: PRO EVOLUTION SOCCER 2018
